en.extract:
The Wangi-wangi white-eye (Zosterops paruhbesar), locally known as sui, is a species of songbird in the white-eye family. It is endemic to a single island, Wangi-wangi Island, in the Wakatobi Islands, Indonesia.
Zosterops paruhbesar has a yellowish head, throat, vent and mid ventral stripe. The flanks are greyish and the flight feathers and tail feathers are brown. Like many other white-eyes, it has a ring of white feathers around the eye. The Wangi-wangi white-eye's bright yellow bill sets it apart from the Wakatobi white-eye, the only other white-eye on Wangi-wangi, as well as other white-eye species in the broader region.


See also
List of bird species described in the 2020s

The '''Wangi-wangi white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops paruhbesar'''''), locally known as '''sui''',<ref>{{Cite web |title=Kamponaone: Surga Burung Sui di Taman Nasional Wakatobi | url=https://hijauku.com/2022/12/19/kamponaone-surga-burung-sui-di-taman-nasional-wakatobi/ |website=hijauku.com |language=id |date=2022-12-19 |access-date=2023-05-27}}</ref> is a species of [[songbird]] in the [[white-eye]] family.<ref>{{Cite Q|Q115755958}}</ref> It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to a single island, [[Wangi-wangi Island]], in the [[Wakatobi Regency|Wakatobi Islands]], [[Indonesia]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=GeoHack - Wangi-wangi Island |url=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Wangi-wangi_Island&params=5.3164_S_123.5817_E_source:wikidata |access-date=2022-10-25 |website=geohack.toolforge.org}}</ref>

''Zosterops paruhbesar'' has a yellowish head, throat, vent and mid ventral stripe. The flanks are greyish and the flight feathers and tail feathers are brown. Like many other white-eyes, it has a ring of white feathers around the eye.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Zosterops palpebrosus - Oriental White-eye - Taxo4254 - Wiki.nus |url=https://wiki.nus.edu.sg/display/TAX/Zosterops+palpebrosus+-+Oriental+White-eye |access-date=2022-10-22 |website=wiki.nus.edu.sg |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Anderson |first=Natali |date=2022-10-21 |title=Newly-Described Bird Species is Endemic to Indonesian Island {{!}} Sci.News |url=https://www.sci.news/biology/zosterops-paruhbesar-11318.html |access-date=2022-10-22 |website=Sci.News: Breaking Science News |language=en-US}}</ref> The Wangi-wangi white-eye's bright yellow bill sets it apart from the [[Wakatobi white-eye]], the only other white-eye on Wangi-wangi, as well as other white-eye species in the broader region.
